standards: PRISMA 2020 (Page et al. 2021, BMJ 372:n71): Identification, Screening, Included
PRISMA-trAIce R1 (Holst et al. 2025, JMIR AI 4:e80247, proposed extension): the flow distinguishes AI decisions from human decisions
method: Every count flagged source.kind 'scripted' is computed by benchmark/scripts/build_flow_model.py from the raw CSVs in benchmark/data/. Counts flagged 'external' are pipeline or audit figures not present under benchmark/data/ and are sourced to knowledge/verification.md. The script asserts the canonical benchmark invariants before writing and refuses to write on any mismatch; regenerate with: python benchmark/scripts/build_flow_model.py.

source.rule: rows of papers_full.csv keyed by Zotero_Key

claimed_source_tool_distribution.scope: Source_Tool is carried only by the human assessment track and is not auditable; the column predates the repository record (knowledge/verification.md, acquisition).

source.rule: Source_Tool column of human_assessment.csv, value counts
restored RIS files mapped via corpus/source_tool_mapping.json; not under benchmark/data/
Is_Duplicate = Yes in papers_full.csv

duplicates_removed_before_screening.note: no identification-phase removal step exists; deduplication happened inside human screening as Duplicate exclusions. The flow is recorded as it happened.

source.rule: no removal step in the data; all identified records enter the screening files

identification.non_auditable: Query dates per provider, provider-side model versions, the executed deep-research prompt instantiations, and the merge/cleaning step are not reconstructable from the repository (knowledge/verification.md).

source.rule: rows of human_assessment.csv keyed by Zotero_Key
Decision = Include in human_assessment.csv
Decision = Exclude in human_assessment.csv

exclusion_reasons.vocabulary_note: values outside benchmark/config/categories.yaml (e.g. Other) and empty cells are a documented hygiene finding (knowledge/verification.md).

source.rule: Exclusion_Reason column over Exclude rows in human_assessment.csv, value counts
Has_HA != Yes in papers_full.csv
Has_HA = Yes in papers_full.csv
Zotero_Keys in human_assessment.csv absent from llm_assessment_10k.csv

human_lane.per_record_reviewer_identity: absent; the CSV has no reviewer column. The track is one consolidated annotation; no inter-human reliability statistic exists (knowledge/verification.md).

ai_lane.role: advisory sibling assessment, stored separately and never merged destructively

source.rule: model snapshot pinned in benchmark/scripts/run_llm_assessment.py; temperature and top-p unrecorded API defaults
rows of llm_assessment_10k.csv keyed by Zotero_Key
Decision = Include in llm_assessment_10k.csv
Decision = Exclude in llm_assessment_10k.csv
Zotero_Keys in llm_assessment_10k.csv absent from human_assessment.csv
intersection of human and AI Zotero_Keys with a decision on both sides
re-paired by Zotero_Key: human Include, AI Include
re-paired by Zotero_Key: human Include, AI Exclude
re-paired by Zotero_Key: human Exclude, AI Include
re-paired by Zotero_Key: human Exclude, AI Exclude
Cohen kappa over the re-paired decisions; interpretation constraints (PABAK, kappa-max, content-only sensitivity) in knowledge/verification.md

open_discrepancy.note: papers_full.csv flags one more humanly assessed record than the benchmark pairs; the surplus key is a stray Has_HA flag absent from the human CSV, so the real pairings stand and no paper is missing.

source.rule: Has_HA = Yes keys minus human_assessment.csv keys
human Include count; the human decision is the binding record
both_include + human_include_ai_exclude; the remaining human includes sit among the human-only records
AI Include count; advisory only, never binding
the both_include confusion cell

reports_and_text_basis.note: PRISMA 2020 asks for reports sought and retrieved. Human screening was not conditioned on retrieval; decisions exist for papers whose text was never acquired. The chain below is the documented acquisition and serving state, not a screening gate, and is not under benchmark/data/.

source.rule: acquisition loss chain
acquisition loss chain
acquisition loss chain
acquisition loss chain
per-paper source markers in docs/data/fulltext_index.json, summarised in knowledge/verification.md

named_gaps: No pre-registered protocol and no pre-specified AI use exist (PRISMA 2020 item 24, trAIce M1); non-reconstructable by definition.
Corpus papers without a human decision (Has_HA = No); for these only an advisory AI assessment exists.
Acquisition provenance is non-auditable beyond the RIS-corroborated records; the executed deep-research prompts were never committed.
A one-record discrepancy between papers_full.csv Has_HA and the benchmark pairing, resolved as a stray flag (see open_discrepancy).
No per-record reviewer identity inside the human track.
Exclusion-reason vocabulary was documented but not enforced (Other values, empty cells).
